name: Fair Housing Council Of Northern New Jersey
description_short: Non-profit fair housing organization providing discrimination reporting, rental/mortgage counseling, and homebuyer education to ensure equal housing access regardless of race, religion, gender, disability, or family status.
description_long:
The Fair Housing Council of Northern New Jersey is a longstanding non-profit organization founded on the principle that fair housing is a legal right. The organization was established after its founder, Lee Porter, and her husband were denied housing in Bergen County in 1965—an experience that motivated her to volunteer and eventually lead the organization as Executive Director starting in 1971. Lee Porter, known as the 'Mother of Fair Housing,' built the Council into a nationally recognized mission for justice and equity in housing access.

The Council offers multiple free or low-cost services designed to protect housing rights and build financial capability. These include discrimination reporting and investigation, rental counseling, mortgage counseling, homebuyer education, and general housing resources. They provide guidance on fair housing laws covering race, color, religion, gender, national origin, disability, and family status protections. The organization also addresses specific issues like sexual harassment in housing and reasonable accommodations for disabilities, including emotional support animals.

What distinguishes this organization is its deep community roots, long operational history since 1971, and focus on underserved populations in Northern New Jersey. The Council provides multilingual materials (English, Spanish, Korean) and maintains an active education program through 'The Good Neighbor' publication. They directly investigate discrimination complaints and connect clients with appropriate resources, making them a practical advocate rather than purely informational.

The main caveat is that this is a regional organization serving Northern New Jersey specifically, so availability is geographically limited. While they offer counseling services, they appear to function primarily as an advocacy and education organization rather than a comprehensive financial counseling provider. Prospective users should confirm they serve their specific area and understand that services focus on housing discrimination and fair housing education rather than broader credit or debt management.
pros: Direct discrimination reporting and investigation services via phone (201-489-3552) and email
HUD-aligned fair housing education covering protected classes and legal rights
Multilingual materials available in English, Spanish, and Korean
Specific counseling services for rental, mortgage, and homebuyer situations
Active case work demonstrated by 2024 success stories (e.g., emotional support animal accommodations)
70+ year organizational history with deep community credibility and founder legacy
Free resources and printed materials addressing discrimination types (sexual harassment, disability, etc.)
cons: Geographic limitation: serves only Northern New Jersey, not a national resource
No indication of online or remote counseling options for out-of-area callers
Limited information about response times for discrimination complaints and investigations
Website does not specify whether all services are free or if some require fees
No details on counselor certifications (e.g., HUD approval status) or staffing qualifications
best_for: Northern New Jersey residents experiencing housing discrimination or denial
First-time homebuyers seeking fair lending education in the region
Renters facing landlord harassment or reasonable accommodation denials
Non-English speakers (Spanish and Korean materials available)
diagnosis: Best for Northern New Jersey residents who have experienced housing discrimination or need fair housing education before renting or buying. Primary caveat: this is a regional non-profit with geographic limitations, and while they investigate discrimination, they are not a comprehensive credit counseling or financial advisory service.
services: Rental counseling and tenant rights education
Fair housing law education and training
Newsletter and community updates via email signup
Civil rights advocacy and policy work
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
